questions.question: Which naming convention is correct for a Java class?
Which of the following is the correct naming convention for a Java method?
How should a constant be declared in Java?
What naming convention do Java packages follow?
Which of the following is a valid single-line comment in Java?
What symbols are used to begin and end a multi-line comment in Java?
What distinguishes a Javadoc comment from a regular multi-line comment?
options: Javadoc comments begin with /** and can generate documentation
Javadoc comments can only be placed inside methods
There is no difference; they are interchangeable
questions.answer: Javadoc comments begin with /** and can generate documentation

questions.question: Which Javadoc tag is used to document a method parameter?
Which Javadoc tag documents what a method sends back to the caller?
What happens if you try to use the reserved word 'class' as a variable name?
options: The program compiles but throws a runtime exception
The compiler issues a warning but allows it
The program will not compile due to a compilation error
The variable is created but cannot be assigned a value
questions.answer: The program will not compile due to a compilation error

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a Java reserved word?
What does the 'final' keyword indicate when applied to a variable?
options: The variable can only be used in the main method
The variable's value cannot be changed after assignment
The variable is automatically set to zero
The variable is deleted after the program ends
questions.answer: The variable's value cannot be changed after assignment

questions.question: What does the 'static' keyword mean when applied to a field or method?
options: It cannot be modified at runtime
It belongs to the class itself, not to any particular instance
It is only accessible within the same package
It must be initialized in the constructor
questions.answer: It belongs to the class itself, not to any particular instance

questions.question: What does the 'public' access modifier do?
options: Makes the element accessible only within its own class
Makes the element accessible only within its package
Makes the element accessible from any other class
Makes the element accessible only to subclasses
questions.answer: Makes the element accessible from any other class

questions.question: Given the code 'String FIRST_NAME = "Alice";', which naming convention is violated?
options: Variables should use camelCase, not UPPER_SNAKE_CASE
questions.answer: Variables should use camelCase, not UPPER_SNAKE_CASE

questions.question: Given the class declaration 'public class bad_conventions', what is the naming violation?
options: Class names cannot contain underscores at all
Class names should use PascalCase (e.g., BadConventions)
Class names must be all uppercase
questions.answer: Class names should use PascalCase (e.g., BadConventions)

questions.question: Which of the following variable declarations follows correct Java naming conventions?
Why do Java naming conventions matter even though the compiler does not enforce them?
options: Because code that violates conventions runs slower
Because they make code readable and consistent for other developers
Because the JVM will throw a warning at runtime
Because naming conventions are required by the Java Language Specification
questions.answer: Because they make code readable and consistent for other developers

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of an unnecessary (useless) comment?
options: // Calculate weighted average of exam scores
// Apply tax rate to determine amount owed
// Check if the grade qualifies for the honor roll

questions.question: Where should a Javadoc comment be placed?
options: At the very end of the file
Inside the method body it documents
Immediately above the class, method, or field it documents
On the same line as the code it documents
questions.answer: Immediately above the class, method, or field it documents

questions.question: Which of the following lines would cause a compilation error?
What is the correct convention for declaring a constant that represents a tax rate in Java?
Which of the following is a Java reserved word?
What is the purpose of the 'return' reserved word in Java?
options: It sends a value back from a method to the caller
It restarts the current loop iteration
questions.answer: It sends a value back from a method to the caller

questions.question: Which statement about Java reserved words is true?
options: Reserved words can be used as variable names if placed in quotes
Reserved words are case-insensitive, so 'Class' and 'class' are both reserved
Reserved words have predefined meanings in Java and cannot be used as identifiers
New reserved words can be created by the programmer using the 'reserve' keyword
questions.answer: Reserved words have predefined meanings in Java and cannot be used as identifiers
